steps.title: Find the structural clue
steps.content: Akana is called a "contemporary" author whose work is ______ by a "rich literary history." The sentence beginning "From the traditional stories of the *Kanaka Maoli*, or Native Hawaiian people, to the works of writers such as David Malo" establishes that this heritage came before her. The blank must describe how that earlier tradition relates to her present-day work.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘preceded’: “preceded by a rich literary history” means the heritage came before her work, placing her within an established tradition. This matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘summarized’: The literary heritage did not condense or sum up her work — she came after it, not the reverse. Violated rule: word meaning incompatible with the passage’s chronology. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘supplanted’: “Supplanted” means replaced or displaced. The passage says the heritage enriches her work, not that it displaced it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘overlooked’: The passage celebrates Akana; saying her work is “overlooked by” the tradition directly contradicts the passage’s tone and content. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
